messages.content:
edit and improve the style of the excerpts from my sci-fi fantasy novel: We say hello to him, but he is furious, and his eyes are
cold steel.
There seems to be a scary storm behind his face that can
come out at any moment and destroy everything and anyone
around him.
Ali and I are surprised, or it is better to say we are
frightened.
“I have never seen him so mad like this before,” Ali
whispers.
“We have always seen him very polite, calm, and
patient,” I reply quietly.

We greet him with a friendly "hello," only to be met with a seething rage that freezes the warmth in his eyes, transforming them into icy, unforgiving steel.
A tempest of fury lurks behind his visage, ready to unleash its destructive power upon anyone unfortunate enough to cross his path.
Ali and I exchange shocked glances, fear creeping into our hearts instead of the anticipated joy.
"Never have I witnessed such wrath in him," Ali breathes, his voice barely above a whisper.
"You're right," I respond, my own words carrying the weight of a secret shared between us. "He has always been the epitome of politeness, serenity, and patience."

We enter a large office where a military officer stands
still and salutes to the colonel on arrival. Then we all move
on to the next smaller room that looks like a safe room.

As we step into the expanse of the office, a military officer stands at attention, his posture unwavering, saluting the colonel with precision upon our arrival. We exchange fleeting nods of acknowledgment before proceeding into the adjacent room, its walls sturdy, giving off an undeniable air of fortification reminiscent of a secure safe room.

Colonel Iranmardy then introduces the army officer:
Major X, UN Army. And then he says, “Major X and his
colleagues are here to train you for the coming missions.”
Next, he turns on the 3D projector on the table, allowing
us to hear and watch some Ghezghazal confessions. In the
video, Ghezghazal was confessing to the grim acts
committed by her and her men.
The crime scene is so shocking that when Ghezghazal
speaks in the film, the UN Army officer turns his head back,
so not to see her face.

Colonel Iranmardy introduces the military officer entrusted with our training, his voice resolute as he declares, "Allow me to present Major X, a seasoned member of the UN Army." His words hang in the air, infusing the room with a sense of anticipation. We instinctively shift our focus towards Major X, acknowledging his presence with reverent nods.

With purposeful intent, Colonel Iranmardy continues, his tone solemn, "These distinguished individuals and their colleagues have been summoned to prepare you for the imminent missions ahead." A wave of anticipation ripples through our hearts as we ponder the gravity of the challenges that await us.

As our attention remains fixed upon the colonel, he activates the 3D projector positioned on the table. The room is bathed in the glowing hues of the projected images, capturing our senses as we bear witness to the confessions of Ghezghazal. Her despicable acts, unveiled before our eyes, elicit shock and revulsion within us.

The gravity of her crimes is so profound that even the stoic Major X instinctively averts his gaze, purposefully turning his head away from the screen. There is an unspoken understanding among us, shared by the Major, to shield himself from the disturbing visage of Ghezghazal as she speaks her vile words.

When Colonel Iranmardy turns off the projector, our
faces, all of us, become as like as the colonel’s face; Hard,
cold, and furious.

As Colonel Iranmardy deactivates the projector, a palpable shift lingers in the air, reflected in the countenances of each person present. Our expressions mirror that of the colonel, hardened by an icy resolve, radiating a frigid determination that matches his own. Anger surges within us, mingling with an unwavering sense of purpose. We are united in our collective fury, prepared to confront the darkness that looms before us.

The UN major is the first to speak, “When one can speak
very indifferently about the crimes she has committed, it
shows that eradicating her organization at the international
level can ultimately be a very complicated task.”

Major X, the UN representative, breaks the silence, his voice carrying a weight of somber realism. "The casual indifference with which she speaks of her heinous crimes only underscores the complexity of eradicating her organization on an international scale." His words resonate with a sense of both caution and acknowledgement of the daunting challenges that lie ahead.

After the meeting ends, Colonel Iranmardy leaves us,
and a security guard opens a tunnel’s door and takes us to
an electric car, and after a few minutes driving, leads us to
a hall where there are a few rooms and a steel door.

As the meeting concludes, Colonel Iranmardy leaves our presence, his departure marking the beginning of a new phase. A security guard orchestrates our passage, guiding us towards a concealed tunnel entrance. With a decisive gesture, the guard opens the door, revealing an awaiting electric car. We step inside, the hum of its engine accompanying us as we embark on a brief journey.

After a few minutes of winding through hidden passages, the guard steers us towards a grand hall, graced with several rooms and a formidable steel door commanding attention. Our destination lies within, cloaked in anticipation and concealed within the depths of that impenetrable doorway.

“I have never seen this section before, but as the
evidence shows, and according to some whispers I have
heard, I guess we are at the bottom of the organization’s
central building Jacket,” says Ali.

As we enter the unfamiliar section of the building, a sense of curiosity and intrigue hangs heavy in the air. Ali, breaking the silence, expresses his astonishment, "I've never laid eyes on this section before. However, based on the evidence and hushed rumors that have reached my ears, I suspect we have descended into the depths of Jacket, the very heart of the organization's central stronghold." His words carry a mix of awe and trepidation, acknowledging the gravity of our surroundings.

Major X releases us for a few minutes and then goes
into one of those rooms.
“I have never seen this section before, but as the
evidence shows, and according to some whispers I have
heard, I guess we are at the bottom of the organization’s
central building Jacket,” says Ali.

Major X grants us a brief reprieve, allowing us a momentary respite before he disappears into one of the rooms. Seizing this opportunity, Ali's voice breaks the silence once again, filled with a mixture of awe and uncertainty. "This is uncharted territory for me as well. However, given the undeniable evidence and the murmurs that have reached my ears, it seems plausible that we have descended to the very depths of Jacket, the organization's central stronghold." His words echo the weight of our clandestine mission, reinforcing the notion that we stand at the precipice of something monumental.

He continues, “Some of my colleagues in emergency
drills have seen that the whole building sinks down into its
jacket so that the tip of the pyramid, the first floor (the
Colonel Iranmardy’s office, the Headquarters) goes to the
lowest level.

Ali's words continue, his voice laced with a mix of apprehension and awe. "In some of the emergency drills my colleagues have participated in, they've witnessed the entire building sinking into its formidable jacket, causing the tip of the pyramid—the very first floor, which houses Colonel Iranmardy's office and serves as the headquarters—to descend to the lowest level." His revelation adds another layer of mystery and intrigue to the already enigmatic surroundings we find ourselves in. The very structure of the building seems to be imbued with secrets and mechanisms beyond our current comprehension.

I ask Ali, “Where are the exits?”
Ali answers, “There are many emergencies exits on
many floors. They open to different tunnels in the jacket,
and probably that steel door is one of them.”

Concerned for our safety, I turn to Ali, seeking clarity amidst the labyrinthine environment. "Where can we find the exits?" I inquire, hoping for a glimmer of reassurance.

Ali responds, his voice tinged with a touch of uncertainty. "There are multiple emergency exits scattered across various floors. These exits lead to different tunnels within the jacket. It is highly likely that the steel door we encountered earlier is one such exit." His words offer a semblance of solace, hinting at the presence of escape routes should we find ourselves in perilous circumstances. Nevertheless, the intricate network of tunnels and exits within the jacket remains shrouded in mystery, perhaps mirroring the intricate nature of the organization itself.

The Major comes back with two other Majors.
Ali says, “Since we don’t know their names correctly,
isn’t it better to call them Majors A to C?”
I hide my smile, but I like Ali’s sense of humor.
Major A, responses quickly and says in a severe tone,
“If the joke is funny and good enough, we speak it out loud,
so everyone can enjoy it.”

As the Major returns accompanied by two others, Ali proposes an idea to simplify addressing them. "Since we aren't familiar with their names, wouldn't it be easier to refer to them as Majors A to C?" Ali suggests, his playful humor shining through. I suppress a smile, appreciating Ali's lighthearted approach amidst the intense atmosphere.

Major A, however, responds with a quick retort, his tone stern, "If a joke is truly amusing and worthy, it deserves to be shared aloud so that everyone can partake in its enjoyment." His words carry an air of seriousness, urging us to focus on the gravity of our mission rather than indulging in levity.

We quickly stand to attention, and the class starts.
One of those Majors teaches us how to fight in the dark,
the other one shows us the electronic traps and how it is
possible to neutralize security systems, and the last one
teaches us about explosive traps, and how to nullify them.

With swift precision, we snap to attention as the class commences. One of the Majors assumes the role of instructor, imparting invaluable knowledge on the art of combat in darkness. We absorb the techniques and strategies, honing our skills to navigate and engage in the absence of light.

Another Major shares insights into the realm of electronic traps, demonstrating the intricacies of bypassing and disabling security systems. Our focus intensifies as we learn the delicate dance between technology and infiltration, understanding the importance of subtlety and precision in evading detection.

Lastly, the remaining Major imparts wisdom regarding explosive traps, unraveling the inner workings and highlighting potential countermeasures. We absorb the knowledge, internalizing the methods to thwart and neutralize these deadly devices, preparing to face the potential dangers that lie ahead.

Throughout the session, we become both students and warriors, honing our abilities in the realms of darkness, technology, and danger, equipping ourselves with the necessary tools to navigate the treacherous path that awaits us.

During training, Ali and I find out that these decorated
veterans have passed many training courses. And as the UN
soldiers, they have earned many medals for their
courageous actions in combat and missions. And perhaps if
they put all their medals and badges on their chests, they
might have to bend over while standing or walking.

As the training progresses, Ali and I come to realize the extensive experience and expertise possessed by these decorated veterans. They have undergone numerous rigorous training courses, demonstrating their commitment to honing their skills. Their dedication and bravery in combat and missions as UN soldiers have earned them a multitude of medals and badges, symbols of their exceptional valor and contributions.

The sheer number of their accolades is staggering, to the extent that one wonders if they were to wear all their decorations on their chests, it might require them to bend slightly while standing or walking. The weight of their accomplishments is tangible, just as the weight of their medals would be if physically displayed. It is a testament to their bravery and dedication, inspiring us to strive for greatness in our own journey towards becoming capable agents in this critical mission.

Classes and training are over after three weeks. Ali and
I have successfully completed the courses.
Now that we have gained certain vital knowledge and
skills by successfully passing the courses and training, our
trainers treat us as one of their corps. And Colonel
Iranmardy, on the recommendation of the veteran Majors,
138
bestows us each the special-forces badge through a small
military ceremony.

After three intense weeks of classes and training, Ali and I emerge as victors, having successfully completed the rigorous courses. With our heads held high, we carry the confidence and knowledge that we have acquired, knowing that we stand amongst the ranks of the skilled and capable.

Our trainers, recognizing our growth and determination, now accept us as members of their esteemed corps. The bond that we forge with them transcends the student-teacher relationship, evolving into a camaraderie built on mutual respect and shared experiences.

In a small military ceremony, Colonel Iranmardy honors our accomplishments based on the glowing recommendation of the veteran Majors. With pride and a sense of achievement, he bestows upon each of us the special-forces badge, symbolizing our dedication, perseverance, and newfound status as esteemed members of this covert unit. The weight of the badge upon our chests serves as a constant reminder of the responsibilities and opportunities that lie ahead, fueling our resolve and driving us forward into the unfolding mission.

Later, Colonel Iranmardy call us and says, “You have
to get ready for a long-term mission abroad.”
Then we go to the 4th-floor dentistry, and they replace
some of our old teeth with newly cloned ones. And in the
electronics department, our devices (camera and
transmitter) get re-tested.

In a moment that fills us with both anticipation and trepidation, Colonel Iranmardy summons us, revealing the next chapter in our journey. His voice carries an air of authority and a weighty sense of purpose as he delivers his directive, "You must prepare yourselves for a long-term mission outside our borders." The gravity of his words settles upon us, igniting a flurry of preparation and readiness.

We make our way to the 4th-floor dentistry, where skilled professionals meticulously replace some of our worn teeth with meticulously cloned counterparts. This procedure ensures that all traces of our identity remain concealed, allowing us to seamlessly blend into the unknown terrain we are soon to navigate.

As we proceed to the electronics department, our trusty devices, including cameras and transmitters, undergo thorough retesting. Skilled technicians subject them to an extensive battery of checks and calibrations, ensuring their reliability and functionality in the face of forthcoming challenges.

Every step taken in preparation for our imminent mission serves as a reminder that we are venturing into uncharted territory, where our skills, resilience, and hidden identities will be put to the test.

The next day, we fly back to the old capital, Tehran, and
then go to the same security building next to the massive
shopping mall to find out about our mission. Colonel David
and Iranmardy receive us in a secured room.

The following day, our journey takes us back to the ancient capital of Tehran. With a sense of familiarity mixed with anticipation, we make our way to the sprawling security building adjacent to the massive shopping mall where we are set to receive crucial information about our mission. Colonel David and Colonel Iranmardy await our arrival, welcoming us into a secure room specially designated for this purpose.

As we step into the room, a sense of gravity settles upon us. We bear witness to the intersection of seasoned experience and unwavering resolve embodied by Colonels David and Iranmardy, acknowledging the critical nature of the task at hand. In this secured sanctuary, surrounded by unwavering dedication and focused determination, we await the unveiling of the details that will guide us through the complexities of our mission.

And then Colonel Iranmardy says, “The details of your
mission, which has animated 3D into an electronic file, is
for you to learn and practice the scheme in various
sequences.” And he adds, “You have a week to learn it, and
as you know, you shouldn’t talk about it to anyone, and the
only place that you can keep the file is in the safe of the
secured room.”

Colonel Iranmardy's voice resonates with a commanding presence as he addresses us, his words carrying the weight of our mission. "The intricate details of your mission, meticulously recorded in an animated 3D electronic file, will serve as your guide for learning and practicing the scheme in various sequences," he declares. The room grows still as we internalize the significance of the information before us.

He continues, emphasizing the importance of secrecy and discretion. "You have one week to diligently study and familiarize yourselves with the contents of the file. It is crucial that you understand the gravity of this information and refrain from discussing it with anyone." His directive hangs heavily in the air, underscoring the need for utmost confidentiality.

Colonel Iranmardy concludes with a reminder about the file's secure storage. "The sole place for safeguarding this file is within the confines of the secure room's safe. It is vital that it remains locked away when not being reviewed, ensuring its protection from unauthorized access." The significance of these instructions settles upon us, amplifying our sense of responsibility and the realization that our mission demands the highest level of discretion and dedication.

Colonel Iranmardy continues again, “Your aim is a
resort in a country adjacent to the region. The headquarters
of these criminals are located there through the confessions
made by Ghezghazal and some of her guests. And consists
of hotels, casinos, sports gambling, all kinds of full-contact
battles between men, robots, modified or cloned animals,
and half-human animals. And also, a variety of zoos where
they breed some of these strange creatures. There are wild
nude shows like the ones you saw at the Ghezghazal house,
and a hospital for organ transplants and cloning the organs.

Colonel Iranmardy's voice resonates with a mix of grim determination and moral outrage as he delves into the details of our mission. "Your objective lies within a resort located in a neighboring country, serving as the headquarters for these criminals," he reveals, his words punctuated by the weight of confessions extracted from Ghezghazal and her associates.

He goes on to describe the sinister nature of the establishment, painting a
